rulings.question: What benchmark harness should sk-doc use after conversion?
rulings.ruling: Use the existing deep-improvement Lane C skill-benchmark harness, stored under .opencode/skills/sk-doc/benchmark/, with router mode as the mandatory CI gate and live mode as the operator/default evidence run.
rulings.rationale: Peer hubs already benchmark against their own manual_testing_playbook across D1 routing, D2 discovery, D3 efficiency, D4 usefulness, and D5 connectivity; router mode replays hub-router.json plus mode-registry.json deterministically, while live mode dispatches through cli-opencode and is the only mode that scores advisor/live usefulness legs.

rulings.question: What must the sk-doc routing corpus cover?
rulings.ruling: Create one deterministic gold scenario for every registered sk-doc workflowMode and every /create:* verb it owns; no registered workflowMode may lack routerSignals, tieBreak coverage, expected resources, and a pass/fail assertion.
rulings.rationale: The canon checker requires routerSignals keys to match the registry workflowMode set bidirectionally and requires routerPolicy.tieBreak to cover every registered mode; the benchmark dimensions then score routing, discovery, efficiency, usefulness, and connectivity against that playbook corpus.

rulings.question: What is the passing routing profile for a workflow-only hub with no surface axis?
rulings.ruling: All sk-doc modes should be packetKind:"workflow" with workflowMode, backendKind, toolSurface, and advisorRouting; the registry should declare no surface-axis extension, and the router should not require surfaceBundle outcomes.
rulings.rationale: The checker accepts hubs with zero surface packets, fails a declared surface-axis extension when there are no surface modes, validates workflowMode/backendKind/toolSurface/advisorRouting on every mode, and only demands surfaceBundle when surfaceCount is greater than zero.

rulings.question: How should the conditional parity gate be defined?
rulings.ruling: Make CONDITIONAL acceptable only for measurement gaps: unscored D1-inter/D4 in router mode or same-mode D3 proxy gaps. Make FAIL mandatory for any canon failure, D1-intra miss, D2 discovery miss, D5 connectivity failure, hubRoute regression, toolSurface violation, routerSignals mismatch, or tieBreak gap.
rulings.rationale: Peer benchmark docs explicitly classify router-mode D3 gaps as measurement gaps and D1-inter/D4 as unscored by design, while peer reports hard-gate D5 and expose hubRoute/toolSurface failures separately; the parent checker makes hub-router, registry consistency, description, playbook, and benchmark baseline canon checks fail by default.

rulings.question: How should discovery and efficiency be measured?
rulings.ruling: Score D2 as expected-resource recall from router replay and observed loads; score D3 as overload/waste, especially routedCount versus wastedCount, but compare D3 only against runs with the same trace mode and corpus shape.
rulings.rationale: The sk-code report records D2 as router-replay-recall, D3 as router-overload with routedCount and wastedCount, and live evidence as observedReads/toolCalls; peer docs warn that D3 movement is meaningful only between runs of the same mode and corpus shape.

rulings.question: How should cross-hub parity be reported?
rulings.ruling: Report sk-doc with the same skill-benchmark-report.v1 JSON/MD pair, dimensionScores, gate block, verdict, aggregateScore, and unscoredDimensions used by sk-code, sk-design, and deep-loop; compare dimensions and gate outcomes, not raw aggregate alone.
rulings.rationale: All inspected peer reports use schemaVersion skill-benchmark-report.v1 with verdict, aggregateScore, gate, dimensionScores, and unscoredDimensions; peer docs keep frozen baselines and instruct comparing against baseline artifacts rather than overwriting them.

rulings.question: What canon conformance checks belong before benchmarking?
rulings.ruling: Run parent-skill-check in strict mode before the benchmark and treat it as a hard preflight: exactly one hub graph-metadata.json, valid mode-registry, direct-child packet dirs, valid hub-router, reverse directory consistency, description.json, manual_testing_playbook, and benchmark baseline must pass.
rulings.rationale: The checker documents the parent hub pattern as one advisor identity with non-discoverable packets, enforces exactly one graph-metadata.json at hub root, validates packet directories, hub-router, reverse directory consistency, description.json, playbook, and benchmark presence, and makes canon checks fail by default unless explicitly opted into WIP warnings.

risks: Raw aggregate parity can mislead because peer router baselines leave D1-inter and D4 unscored by design and may have D3 measurement gaps; gate on D1-intra/D2/D5/canon plus explicitly labeled CONDITIONAL gaps instead. Evidence: .opencode/skills/sk-design/benchmark/README.md:34; .opencode/skills/sk-design/benchmark/README.md:37; .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/benchmark/README.md:39

The current sk-code live-final benchmark is only CONDITIONAL with aggregateScore 71, D1-intra 92, D3 42, and D5 100, so sk-code is stronger as an artifact/layout reference than as the minimum quality target. Evidence: .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final/skill-benchmark-report.json:11; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final/skill-benchmark-report.json:12; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final/skill-benchmark-report.json:23; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final/skill-benchmark-report.json:31; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final/skill-benchmark-report.json:40

If sk-doc declares surface-axis with zero surface packets, the canon checker fails; the no-surface design must stay strictly workflow-only. Evidence: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:423; .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:424
open: Confirm whether create-benchmark and create-changelog stay as workflowModes before freezing routerSignals and tieBreak coverage; the checker requires routerSignals and tieBreak to cover every registered workflowMode. Evidence: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:549; .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:590

Decide whether sk-doc needs a live-final smoke subset for all /create:* verbs or only for the highest-risk verbs; live mode is the operator default for true routing verdicts and is the path that scores live/advisor/usefulness legs. Evidence: .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/README.md:23; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/README.md:58; .opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/README.md:65
